commands.review_summary:
Retries replay-safe `stopReason="error"` assistant turns that emitted only non-visible reasoning content, tightens terminal-state guards, and adds focused unit plus QA-lab mock Anthropic coverage.

PR surface: Source +210, Tests +372, Docs +99. Total +681 across 10 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main's retry guard rejects the reported thinking-content plus non-zero-output shape, and the sibling reasoning-only retry helper returns `null` for errored assistant turns; I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Maintainer review should decide the provider-proof and message-delivery replay risk; I found no narrow automated repair to queue.

target.title: fix(agents): retry thinking-only errored turns
target.body:
Summary
- Fixes #91953.
- Retries replay-safe `stopReason="error"` turns when the assistant emitted only non-visible thinking content before the provider error.
- Runs that retry before assistant failover can surface the same non-visible provider error as a terminal failure.
- Keeps the existing safety boundary: no retry after potential side effects, no retry for auth/rate-limit/billing/format/image failures, no retry when visible assistant text exists, and no retry for empty error turns with non-zero output unless the content is thinking-only.

Verification
- `pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.empty-error-retry.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts`
- `pnpm test extensions/qa-lab/src/providers/mock-openai/server.test.ts`
- `pnpm openclaw qa suite --provider-mode mock-openai --model anthropic/claude-opus-4-8 --alt-model anthropic/claude-opus-4-8 --scenario anthropic-thinking-error-recovery-replay-safe-read --concurrency 1 --output-dir .artifacts/pr-92191-anthropic-thinking-error-e2e-3`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm tsgo:core` and `pnpm tsgo:extensions` still fail on the existing `src/agents/sessions/sdk.ts:279` `ThinkingCatalogEntry` compatibility error.

Real behavior proof
- behavior: A replay-safe Anthropic-style assistant turn that ends as a provider error after emitting only signed thinking content is retried by the embedded runner instead of surfacing `FailoverError: An unknown error occurred`.
- environment: Local source checkout plus this branch, Node 22.22.2, pnpm 11.2.2, isolated OpenClaw QA home, mock-openai provider mode, Anthropic mock model `anthropic/claude-opus-4-8`, qa-channel + qa-lab bus + real gateway child.
- steps: Added a QA scenario that runs through the gateway, performs a replay-safe read, returns an Anthropic Messages stream with signed `thinking` content, `usage.output_tokens=1120`, and a terminal stop mapped to runtime `stopReason="error"`, then verifies the same-prompt retry completes with `ANTHROPIC-THINKING-ERROR-RECOVERED-OK`.
- evidence: Before moving the retry before assistant failover, the new E2E failed with `agent.wait returned error: FailoverError: An unknown error occurred`. After the fix, the E2E passes: 1 scenario, 0 failures, final marker `ANTHROPIC-THINKING-ERROR-RECOVERED-OK`, `requests=4`.
- observedResult: The runner now retries the non-visible thinking-only provider error before assistant failover claims it, while focused regression tests keep side-effect, visible-progress, retry-budget, auth/failover, and empty-response boundaries covered.
- notTested: I did not replay the original intermittent live Vertex AI / Claude Opus provider failure because this local setup does not have the exact live provider path configured and the upstream error is not deterministically fault-injectable. The added gateway E2E exercises the same runtime retry boundary with an Anthropic Messages stream shaped after the issue.
target.author: ai-hpc
